Hero slider menu DAC1AE73-284B-459C-B9CE-4865C248F4D0 اكتشف مجال تخصصك جميع البرامج قدّم قدّم طلبك الآن مواعيد التقديم النهائية تعرّف على الجامعة حياة الطلّاب المكتبة قدّم الآن للعام الأكاديمي 2026–2027 يمكن للطلاب التقديم على مجموعة واسعة من برامج الماجستير والبكالوريوس والدبلوم المتاحة ضمن كليات الجامعة الخمس.اطّلع على متطلبات القبول، والمواعيد النهائية المهمة، وتفاصيل اختبارات تحديد المستوى، والإرشادات اللازمة لإتمام طلبك خطوة بخطوة.احجز مقعدك وابدأ مسيرتك الأكاديمية في جامعة الدوحة للعلوم والتكنولوجيا. كيفية التقديم إلى جامعة الدوحة للعلوم والتكنولوجيا قدّم الآن على برامج الماجستير من المختبرات المتطورة والبرامج الأكاديمية المرتبطة باحتياجات سوق العمل، إلى التعلّم التطبيقي، وفرص البحث العلمي، والشراكات العالمية، توفّر جامعة الدوحة للعلوم والتكنولوجيا بيئة أكاديمية متكاملة تهدف إلى إعدادك لمسار مهني ناجح وتمكينك من إحداث أثر حقيقي في مجال تخصصك. قدم الآن هل أنت طالب جديد في جامعة

الدوحة للعلوم والتكنولوجيا وتبحث عن الدعم؟ توفر جامعة الدوحة للعلوم والتكنولوجيا مجموعة شاملة من الخدمات والبرامج، ابتداءً من الإرشاد الأكاديمي والتوجيه المهني وصولاً إلى الأندية الطلابية وخدمات الصحة والأنشطة الجامعية، وذلك لدعم نجاحك وضمان تجربة جامعية غنية ومتكاملة. اقرأ المزيد قدم الآن لاختبار IELTS اتخذ الخطوة التالية في مسيرتك الأكاديمية من خلال التسجيل لاختبار IELTS في جامعة الدوحة للعلوم والتكنولوجيا. يُعد هذا الاختبار الدولي المعترف به معيارًا لقياس كفاءتك في اللغة الإنجليزية، حيث يقيّم مهارات القراءة والكتابة والاستماع والمحادثة، ويساعدك على استيفاء متطلبات القبول وتحقيق أهدافك التعليمية. كما توفّر جامعة الدوحة للعلوم والتكنولوجيا الإرشاد والموارد اللازمة لضمان استعدادك الجيد وثقتك الكاملة يوم الاختبار. قدم الآن Responsive Video جولة افتراضية في الجامعة استكشفوا حرمنا الجامعي الحديث، الممتد على مساحة 150،000 متر مربع، حيث تعمّ الروح الابتكارية في أنحاء مرافق مميزة وبيئة مليئة بالحيوية. مع أكثر من 10،300 خريج وخريجة، وعدد طلاب حاليين يعادل ذلك، يُشكّل مجتمعنا نسيجًا ثريًا من التنوع، يضمّ فرادًا من أكثر من 86 جنسية.نقدّم مجموعة متنوعة من 76 برنامجًا عبر خمس كليات، حيث نلتزم بالتميز الأكاديمي والتنمية الشاملة. يُحقّق هيكلنا التدريسي المتفاني، الذي يضمّ أكثر من 250 عضوًا متمرّسًا دوليًا، الخبرة والشغف في التدريس، مما يعزّز بيئة للتعلّم والنمو. التفوّق الأكاديميّ جميع البرامج كلية الأعمال تتميز برامج كلية الأعمال بأنها تضم ريادة الأعمال باعتبارها جزءًا لا يتجزأ من برامج الدراسة مما أدى إلى... كلية العلوم الصحية تُعَتَّبَر كلية العلوم الصحية في جامعة الدوحة للعلوم والتكنولوجيا من المؤسسات القطرية الرائدة في تطوير برامج صحية عالمية... كلية الهندسة والتكنولوجيا كلية الهندسة والتكنولوجيا هي الكلية المختارة للهندسة التطبيقية والتعليم التقني في المنطقة، وهي معروفة بالتميز الأكاديمي والبحث التطبيقي... كليّة الحوسبة وتكنولوجيا المعلومات تم تصميم برامج كلية الحوسبة وتكنولوجيا المعلومات لتلبي المعايير المُعترَف بها دوليًّا، وتغطي البرامج مجموعة واسعة من المعارف... كلية التعليم العام تقدم كلية التعليم العام مجموعة من البرامج التمهيدية المشتركة لطلاب جميع برامج البكالوريوس مجمعة في ست مجموعات مواضيعية... وحدة البرنامج التأسيسي انضم إلى البرنامج التأسيسي لتضمن أنك جاهز على المستوى الأكاديمي والشخصي للتقدم لإحدى الدبلومات أو الدرجات العلمية في... برامجنا الجديدة ماجستير التنفيذي في الإدارة الصحية ماجستير العلوم في القبالة بكالوريوس العلوم في صحة الحيوان و العلوم البيطرية دبلوم في صحة الحيوان و العلوم البيطرية آخر الأخبار تصفح جميع الأخبار فبراير 23, 2026 جامعة الدوحة للعلوم والتكنولوجيا تعلن عن فتح باب القبول لفصل الخريف الدراسيّ 2026 واطلاق برامج جديدة يسرّ جامعة الدوحة للعلوم والتكنولوجيا أن تعلن عن فتح باب القبول لفصل الخريف الدراسي 2026 فبراير 12, 2026 جامعة الدوحة للعلوم والتكنولوجيا تستعرض ابتكارات زراعية ونماذج تطبيقية في معرض قطر الزراعي الدوليّ شاركت جامعة الدوحة للعلوم والتكنولوجيا في معرض قطر الزراعي الدولي، حيث استعرضت مجموعة من المشاريع فبراير 10, 2026 جامعة الدوحة للعلوم والتكنولوجيا تحتفي باليوم الرياضي للدولة 2026 من خلال نشاطات تفاعليّة احتفلت جامعة الدوحة للعلوم والتكنولوجياباليوم الرياضي للدولة 2026 من خلال فعالية حيوية، ركّزت على الرياضة، فبراير 7, 2026 جامعة الدوحة للعلوم والتكنولوجيا تسلّط الضوء على التنوّع الثقافي في مهرجان الثقافات 2026 استضافت جامعة الدوحة للعلوم والتكنولوجيا مهرجان الثقافات السنوي، في احتفالية جسّدت التنوّع وعزّزت توجهها نحو فبراير 4, 2026 جامعة الدوحة للعلوم والتكنولوجيا تستعرض حضورها الدولي وبرامج التبادل الطلابي في مؤتمر "إديو كون" شاركت جامعة الدوحة

للعلوم والتكنولوجيا في مؤتمر "إديو كون"، أحد المؤتمرات الدولية الرائدة في مجال فبراير 1, 2026 جامعة الدوحة للعلوم والتكنولوجيا تسلّط الضوء على الابتكار وريادة الأعمال والذكاء الاصطناعي في قمة الويب تشارك جامعة الدوحة للعلوم والتكنولوجيا في قمة الويب من خلال عرض متكامل للشركات الناشئة التي السابقالتالي الفعاليات القادمة تقويم الفعاليات أبريل07 - 12 أسبوع الدوحة للرعاية الصحية أبريل09 مهرجان اليوم العالمي للتوعية بإمكانية الوصول أبريل10 - 11 بطولة مناظرات قطر المفتوحة تطور ونمو + 0 80 برنامجاً 0 8903 عدد الطلاب المسجلين حالياً 0 11016 عدد الخريجين + 0 86 عدد الجنسيات جديد في حرم الجامعة يوهب حاضنة الأعمال المكرسة لتمكين روح الريادة لدى طلاب الجامعة، حيث يحولون أفكارهم المبتكرة إلى حقائق ملموسة. الخدمات المركزية الطلابية وحدة متخصصة في مساعدة طلاب الجامعة ودعمهم في مسيرتهم الأكاديمية والمهنية مركز المهارات المهنية يُساهم في تنمية الكوادر البشرية، بالإضافة إلى تنظيم المهن التقنية التي تتطلب مستوى محدد من المعرفة والمهارات والكفاءات. السابقالتالي تعرف على الجامعة تقديم طلب القبول الحياة في الجامعة زيارة الحرم الجامعي Social media links تويتر يوتيوب انستغرام فيسبوك لينكدين جامعة الدوحة للعلوم والتكنولوجيا – قطر منطقة الطرفة، شارع جليعة شمال الدحيل ص.ب 24449 الدوحة – قطر القائمة الرئيسية: القائمة الرئيسية التسجيل والقبول الشؤون الأكاديمية الحياة في الجامعة الأبحاث حول الجامعة روابط سريعة: القائمة الرئيسية الطلّاب الحاليّون التعليم المستمر والمهني الخرّيجون مركز الاختبارات الوظائف اتصل بنا استكشف دليل الحرم الجامعي قائمة السفلي سياسة الخصوصية الأحكام والشروط جامعة الدوحة للعلوم والتكنولوجيا – قطر @ 2026 الرجوع للاعلي

Instagram Facebook Linkedin Current Student Academic Calendar 2025-2026 Request Letters and Records As a UDST student, you may need certain letters or official documentation related to your student record and financial matters. Our comprehensive range of services includes requests for Diploma/Certificates, graduation letters, student name change, and much more. Use these services to ensure your records are always accurate and up to date. make a request Student Central Services Student Central Services is a hub dedicated to assisting and empowering students as they navigate their academic and professional journeys. Its services include academic advising and support, peer torturing, career services, and many more workshops and programs that are designed to guide and support your success at UDST. Learn more Student Systems UDST has a wide range of IT

systems and applications designed to streamline and enhance your academic and personal tasks. Discover more about essential platforms like D2L, email, and how to submit facilities requests. Learn More Student Handbook This is a comprehensive document containing UDST policies and important information for new and returning students, such as details about internet access, available student services, Student rights, responsibilities, and much more. view the student handbook Important Policies Code of Conduct UDST is a community of students and staff involved in learning, teaching, research and other activities, both on campus and online. All members of the University Community are expected to conduct themselves in a manner that contributes positively to an environment in which integrity, quality, innovation, collaboration, accountability, and sustainability are valued, so as to assure the success of both the individual and the community. The Student Conduct Policy reflects a concern for these values and tries to ensure that members of the University Community can make use of and enjoy the activities, facilities, and benefits of the University without undue interference from others. UDST’s Student Conduct Policy outlines the fundamental rights of students and sets out student responsibilities and standards of student conduct that are required of the University’s students and the University’s expectations with respect to student behavior. It also provides a framework for the management of student misconduct. Dress Code Students are required to dress in a way that respects the local culture. The Qatari national dress for men is the thobe, and for women, it is the abaya. If the Qatari national dress is not worn, the University dress code requires students to wear “business casual” clothing. Looser clothing that covers the knees and arms to the elbow must be chosen. If a student does not follow the dress code, the University may take action that affects him/her. Mobile Phone Etiquette Mobile phones must be turned to silent or off when students enter class. When a mobile phone rings during class, it bothers other classmates and the instructors. Pictures may not be taken of others unless they give permission to do so. Harassment What is harassment? Harassment means to be aggressive towards others or to frighten, disturb or bother others. At UDST we believe everyone has the right to go through their day free from any kind of harassment. The University has a policy to protect you called the UDST Harassment Policy and Procedure. This policy assures all employees, students, guests, vendors and clients the right to work, study and enjoy related activities harassment-free. This applies across all University-owned or operated property and facilities. In other words, there is no place for harassment anywhere on the UDST campus. The policy language below may be difficult for you to understand. But if your feelings tell you that you are being or have been harassed, come and talk to us in private. You can see the student counsellor or come to Student Affairs in Building 6. The UDST Harassment Policy explains that harassment includes any of the

following: Sexual Harassment This includes sexual comments, gestures or physical contact that the individual knows, or should reasonably know, to be unwelcome, unacceptable or offensive. Sexual harassment is not wanted, one-sided and/or hurtful. Discriminatory Harassment Based on race, religion, religious creed, age, sex, sexual orientation, marital status, family status, physical or mental disability, political opinion, colour, or ethnic national or social origin that is directed at, is offensive to, undermines performance, and/or threatens an individual’s economic livelihood. Personal Harassment Occurs when an individual uses his/her authority or position to undermine, sabotage or otherwise adversely affect the career or program of another person. It includes, but is not limited to, repeated offensive comments and/ or actions that demean and belittle an individual and/or cause personal humiliation. It may also include threatening to hurt others, trying to get money from others, forcing others to do something they do not want to do, and/or the filing of unproven and/or hurtful claims of wrongdoing. Sponsorship Opportunities If you have a career goal and wish to be a sponsored student studying at UDST, you are encouraged to contact the Human Resource Departments and Training Centers in all sectors (government, industry and private sector), or you can ask the sponsorship team. Create your own opportunity to become a highly skilled professional, ready to assume a profession in the Qatari workforce. Email: Sponsor@udst.edu.qa Office: 4495 2650 Read More Academic Awards UDST offers opportunities to students to receive a number of academic awards. The academic awards do not require an application and are determined based on academic performance in terms of students’ weighted averages. Some academic awards are determined based on academic performance coupled with other specific criteria such as contribution to university life, faculty recommendation, etc. Full information about academic awards is available at the Admissions & Registration Directorate. Registration Information Click below to find out more about the registration at UDST: Registration Video Registration User Manual Common Registration Errors CLICK HERE TO REGISTER Class Schedules Please refer to the below class schedules to assist you in choosing your classes for Spring 2026:College of BusinessCollege of Computing and ITCollege of Engineering and TechnologyCollege of Engineering and Technology (Trade)College of General EducationCollege of Health SciencesFoundation ProgramNOTE: Classes are subject to change Other Important Dates First Day of Classes: Sunday, May 3, 2026Add/Drop Period: Sunday, May 3, 2026 - Thursday, May 7, 2026Last day to Withdraw with Full Fees Refund: Thursday, May 7, 2026Last day to Withdraw with Pro-Rated Refund: Thursday, May 21, 2026Last day to Withdraw: Thursday, May 21, 2026 Tuition and Fees Check below for tuition and fee information Tuition and Fees Registration FAQ's What is Self-Service Registration? Self-Service Registration is UDST's method of registration implemented in Spring 2022 whereby students can enroll in and manage their classes through their Student Self-Service portal. It allows students to do the following during the Registration Period for each semester: Plan their courses for the current and future semesters Enroll in courses Select which section of a course in which to enroll (if multiple sections are available) Drop courses Swap sections of a course View their schedule Pay tuition and fees How do I register using Self-Service Registration? Self-Service Registration is available to eligible students through their Student Self-Service portal during the Registration Period for each semester. For instructions on how to use Self-Service Registration, please go to the Registration page of the UDST website. Please refer to the Registration User Manual and/or Registration Video for detailed instructions to assist you. If I don't want to register online, am I able to register in person? Registration is only available through the Student Self-Service portal. However, if you have questions related to your program and/or course selection, you may book an appointment with your Academic Advisor or the Advising team through Navigate 360, visit Student Central Services Academic Advising office in Building 13, or email advising@udst.edu.qa . What do I need to register? Students will need access to their Student Self-Service portal, as well as their login information (Student ID and password). What is my User ID? Your User ID is the Student ID number that was assigned to you when you applied to UDST. It is printed on your Student ID card. If you are a new student, you will find your Student ID number in the email you received after you submitted your application. What should I do if I have forgotten my password? If you have forgotten your password, go to the Student Self-Service portal and click Reset Password to send an SMS to your mobile number on your student record. When can I register for my classes? Registration begins prior to each semester and is open until the last day to register published on the Registration page. The Registration Schedule will be published on the Registration page

of the UDST website, and notification of registration opening will be sent to students via SMS. I tried to register online through Self-Service Registration but was not able to. What is the problem? There can be a number of reasons that Self-Service Registration is not available for you:POSSIBLE REASONSWHAT YOU CAN DO ABOUT ITYou may be entering the wrong Student ID and/or passwordIf you do not remember your Student ID, please contact the Admissions and Registration Directorate by calling 4495-2225, Sunday-Thursday 7:30am-3:00pm (9:00am-2:00pm during Ramadan) or emailing RegistrationHelp@udst.edu.qa .You tried to register before your eligible dateThe date registration opens for you is based on the number of credits you have earned in your current program. Once registration opens for your level, it will remain open until the last day to register. Check this page of the website for your registration opening date.Your student record is not activeYou may not yet be admitted, or you may have previously been Academically Dismissed or Discontinued and your student file is no longer active. Please check with the Admissions and Registration Directorate to identify the reason for inactivation and to determine if you are eligible for admission or re-admission. Call 4495-2225, Sunday-Thursday 7:30am-3:00pm (9:00am-2:00pm during Ramadan) or email RegistrationHelp@udst.edu.qa .You have a registration 'hold' on your recordYou may have outstanding admission requirements that must be met, or there may be some other reason you are not eligible to enroll. To identify the reason for the 'hold', return to the Student Homepage in your Student Self-Service portal and click the Tasks tile. The 'hold' must be resolved before enrolling.You have completed all Foundation Program requirements but do not yet see your credit program structureStudents who have completed all Foundation Program requirements need to be transferred to the program originally applied for after Final Grade processing has been completed. The date your program should be available for registration is listed in the Registration Schedule on the Registration page of the website.You do not have a program structure assigned to you in the Self-Service Registration systemIf you are a visiting student or are required to take bridging courses prior to being admitted to a Master's program (Pre-Master's), you will not be assigned to a specific program of study and will need to have your course(s) added manually for you by the appropriate College. I received an error message when trying to add/drop/swap sections of a course. What does the error message mean, and what should I do? There can be a number of reasons for error messages. Please refer to the Common Registration Errors document on this page to find out why you are getting the error message and what you need to do to resolve the issue. I have questions about which courses to take this semester. Is there anyone who can help me? Academic Advising is available through Student Central Service (SCS) Academic Advising or your assigned faculty academic advisor (viewable in your Student Self-Service portal). If you have questions related to your program and/or

course selection, you may book an appointment with your Academic Advisor or the Advising team through Navigate 360 or visit Student Central Services Academic Advising office in Building 13 to receive in-person drop-in advising. Advising questions can also be emailed to advising@udst.edu.qa . When are fees due? Non-sponsored Students will have time until the end of the current semester to settle their accounts. Unfortunately, students who do not complete full payment will not be able to register for the following semester or attend classes. Sponsored students do not need to pay as long as their sponsoring company has sent the details of their sponsorship to the Sponsorship Specialist at the Admissions and Registration Directorate before the start of registration. I don't have a credit card. How can I pay online? To ensure online payment is easier for student, you may pay with your credit card or debit card. We also offer students the opportunity to enroll now and pay later. At the time of Self-Service Registration, you may pay in part or in full, or you may defer your payment to a later date. You can also come in to the Finance Office to pay in person. Cash, however, is not accepted. What if I cannot afford to pay my fees at the time of registration? Non-sponsored students may choose to pay their fees for the current semester at a later date, provided it is before the end of the term. Post-dated cheques will be accepted for students who have requested and received prior approval from management to defer payment. When is the first day of classes? The full semester schedule and deadline dates can be found in the Academic Calendar which is published on our website and on your MyUDST page. Important dates are also posted on the Important Information tile in the Student Self-Service portal. What are the deadlines for adding or dropping a course? The full semester schedule and deadline dates can be found in the Academic Calendar which is published on our website and on your MyUDST page. Important dates are also posted on the Important Information tile in the Student Self-Service portal. How can I add or drop a course? You can add and/or drop courses through the Manage Classes tile in the Student Self-Service portal. Please refer to the Registration User Manual and/or Registration Video for detailed instructions to assist you. If you have questions about the policies regarding adding or dropping courses, please contact Student Central Services Academic Advising Unit ( Advising@udst.edu.qa ). Am I able to change my course section? You can swap course sections up to a maximum of four times per course through the Manage Classes tile in the Student Self-Service portal. Please refer to the Registration User Manual and/or Registration Video for detailed instructions to assist you. Where can I view my class schedule? As soon as you enroll, you may view your class schedule through the Manage Classes tile in the Student Self Service portal. Please refer to

the Registration User Manual and/or Registration Video for detailed instructions to assist you. What books do I need, and from where can I purchase them? All books and class materials can be purchased at the campus bookstore in A23 Campus Corner. To find out what books are required, please check with your instructor or refer to your Course Syllabus. Instagram Facebook Linkedin Current Student Student Systems Distance Learning D2L is the Learning Management System used by UDST to manage the delivery of your courses. How do I access D2L? Enter the D2L link in the address bar of your web browser Enter your 60XXXXX ID number and password Click Log In D2L MyUDST MyUDST is UDST’s Intranet system where you can access many student resources. How do I access the MyUDST homepage? Enter MYUDST link in the address bar of your web browser Enter your 60XXXXXX ID number and password Select UDST - Student & Alumni from the drop down Click Sign In MYUDST PeopleSoft PeopleSoft is UDST’s Student Information System that contains all your biographical details and academic information. How do I access PeopleSoft Self-Service to

view my Student Center? Enter PeopleSoft link in the address bar of your web browser Enter your 60XXXXXX ID number and password Click Sign In PeopleSoft Password Reset UDST’s Password Reset Portal can be used if you have forgotten your password or are having issues logging in. What if I forget my password? Enter Forgot Password link in the address bar of your web browser When prompted, enter your 60XXXXXX number and click Next Enter your QID number and click Next An SMS will be sent to the mobile number on file in PeopleSoft Enter the verification number from the SMS and click Next Enter the new password twice and click Next Send an email to ithelpdesk@udst.edu.qa , including a picture of your student ID, asking to reset the password IT Helpdesk will reset your password and send it to you Password reset Student Email Students can access their email from any desktop, laptop, or mobile device on or off campus. How do I access my UDST email? Enter Outlook link in the address bar of your web browser Enter your email address – 60XXXXXX@udst.edu.qa – and click Next Enter your password and click Sign In Outlook Facility Requests (FAMIS) Facilities helpdesk system allows students to enter facilities service requests and track statuses directly via an easy-to-use self-service interface. FAMIS Self-Service only handles non-emergency requests. For services that require immediate attention to maintain safety or life safety emergencies, please call the Security Control at 4495-2999. If you have any questions regarding work orders or service requests, please facilitieshelpdesk@udst.edu.qa How do I access my UDST email? Enter FAMIS in the address bar of your web browser Enter your Student ID – 60XXXXXX Enter your password and click Login What if I have any problems logging into FAMIS? Send an email to ithelpdesk@udst.edu.qa , including a picture of your student ID, and describing the issue you are facing. FACILTY REQUESTS One Drive Students can access their saved documents in Office 365 through OneDrive. How do I access OneDrive (Z:\Drive is migrated to OneDrive) Enter Office link in the address bar of your web browser Enter your student email address - 60XXXXXX@udst.edu.qa - in the field provided and press Next Enter your password and click sign in Click on the OneDrive icon at the top of the page How do I use OneDrive? All Z:\ drive files can be found under the My Documents folder To create new files and save them to OneDrive, click on the New button from the menu and select the type of file you want to create. You can create Word, Excel, PowerPoint or OneNote files. You can also create new folders. If you have files or folders on your computer that you would like to save in your OneDrive, you can click the Upload button and then select either files or folders. Office Navigate 360 Navigate360 is UDST’s Student Success Management System. Navigate 360 is your digital one-stop shop. View your events and to-do’s, schedule an appointment,

view your class schedule, access resources, and much more. To access Navigate 360: Download from the App Store Download from Google Play Access on your PC Log in with your UDST credentials to get started. Instagram Facebook Linkedin Current Student Attendance Attendance and Punctuality Standards The University recognizes that regular Attendance and participation in Class is fundamental to Student success. The maximum allowable limit for absenteeism is 15% of Learning Sessions per Course during a semester. Students exceeding the allowable limit for absenteeism in a Course will receive a failing grade for that Course, regardless of their performance. The Academic Units may identify specific Learning Sessions, absenteeism from which will lead to a failing grade in the corresponding Course. The University expects all Students to be on time for Learning Sessions. Learning Sessions will commence as listed in the Student’s schedule. Students arriving after commencement of the Learning Session or departing prior to the completion of the Learning Session may be recorded as “Absent”

in the Attendance System at the discretion of the Academic Member. For safety and operational reasons, the Academic Member may, for certain laboratory Learning Sessions, prohibit Students from entering after the Learning Session has commenced. Students arriving late may be prevented from entering the Learning Session and will be recorded as “Absent” in the Attendance System. All non-attendance will be factored into the Student’s attendance computation. Attendance and punctuality standards will be included in the Course Syllabus and communicated to students by Academic Members on their first day of the Course. Attendance RecordingAttendance recording and calculation starts after the end of the Add/Drop period and ends on the last Learning Session of that Course. Academic Members must record Student Attendance into the Attendance System within 24 hours of the Learning Session.Attendance absence percentage is calculated per Course based on the total amount of time the Course is scheduled to meet for the Semester.Attendance will be tracked by swiping or tapping your student ID card when entering any class/lab. It is therefore required that you have your student ID card with you at all times. The following outlines some important information regarding the use of card swiping to record attendance:Swipe to Record Attendance: Failure to swipe will result in being marked absent.No Swipe-Out Required: Attendance is recorded based on swiping in only.Proper Swiping: Remove your ID card from wallets, pouches, or mobiles to avoid swipe errors.Verify Swipe: Confirm successful swiping with a beep or blinking LED and check your attendance in the "Student Attendance Status" tile on your PeopleSoft Student Homepage.Timing: Swiping starts 10 minutes before a class/lab begins. Swipes made earlier will not be recorded.Back-to-Back Sessions: Swipe in again for consecutive sessions in the same room.Additional Notes:Faculty may also conduct attendance checks at any time and update attendance manually.Violations of attendance recording may result in disciplinary action as per the Student Conduct Policy.For technical issues with your student ID card, please contact the IT Helpdesk in Building 10 or email [email protected] .To obtain a new student ID card or replace a lost or damaged one, please contact the Security Office in Building 9, Room 9.1.80.For additional information on attendance recording or if you think there is an error in your absence percentage, please contact your instructor directly. AbsenceStudents who have an extenuating circumstance for being absent must submit their request and appropriate supporting documentation to the Admissions and Registration Directorate in Student Self-Service through Student Homepage > E-Services > Attendance Absence Request within two business days of the Student’s return to the Learning Session. Extenuating circumstances include:Medical notes validated by the Ministry of Public HealthBereavement for spouse, parent, grandparent, child, or siblingMaternityChronic illnessExtenuating circumstances will be recorded in the Attendance System as a Documented Absence and will be included in the Student’s Total Absence percentage. However, only Non-Documented Absence is factored towards the student's 15% maximum allowable limit for absenteeism.Absence from a Learning Session does not relieve Students from completing any missed Course Requirements. The Academic Member may grant

Students an extension for completion of Course Requirements if substantiating evidence is provided. Placements (Clinical and Work Terms) Students, who are enrolled in a Placement as part of their Program, are responsible for ensuring that they adhere to the attendance standards set by the Placement provider. Students exceeding the allowable limit for absenteeism in a Course set by the Placement provider, where it is lower than the standard set by this Policy, will receive a failing grade for that Course. Absenteeism Notification Throughout the semester, notification will be provided to Students who exceed 10% absenteeism. This data will be shared with the Academic Units and Sponsors. These students will be advised to meet with their Academic Advisors who will recommend an appropriate course of action. Where it is reasonable or foreseeable to expect that the 15% maximum allowable limit for absenteeism will be exceeded, Students, in consultation with their Academic Advisor, will be advised to Withdraw from the Course by the end of the Withdrawal Period. Up to and including the last day of the Learning Session, notification will be provided to Students who have failed the Course by exceeding the 15% maximum allowable limit for absenteeism. Students who fail a Course for absenteeism may remain in the Course but will not be eligible to attend the Final Examination in that Course. Academic Members will be notified to assign an Attendance Fail (AF) Final Grade to Students who fail the Course for exceeding this maximum allowable limit. Attendance Review Students who feel that their Attendance has not been accurately recorded in the Attendance System should discuss the matter with the Academic Member teaching that Learning Session. This should be done within five business days of the Learning Session in question. If the discussion with the Academic Member does not result in a satisfactory resolution, the Student may request that the matter be reviewed by the Department Head. This should be done within two business days of the Academic Member’s decision. If the Student is not satisfied with the decision from the Department Head, the matter may be escalated to the Dean or Manager of the Academic Unit for a final decision. Such a request should be made within three business days of the Department Head’s decision. Petition In extenuating and unforeseeable circumstances, Students who exceed the 15% maximum allowable limit for absenteeism can petition the Attendance Fail (AF) Final Grade to the Department Head. Careful consideration will be given to the extenuating nature of the circumstance and when the absenteeism occurred in the Semester. Successful petitions will result in the Student being Withdrawn from the Course and a Final Grade of W assigned. No refund will be issued. In certain extenuating and Documented Absence circumstances, the Dean may grant an exception to students who have exceeded 15% absence. Graduation Ceremony Graduation Requirements Ramadan Schedule The final examination schedule and locations will be available to all students in their Student Self-Service portal and through MYUDST. Students should read and be familiar with all final examination policies. Winter 2026 Final Exam Schedule Foundation, Undergraduate, and Graduate To view exam schedule Click here Final Exam Rules for Students All exams will start on time • Arrive at campus 1 hour before your exam time (to avoid queuing delays at gate). • Go to exam room 30 minutes before your exam. • If you are late for the exam, you will not be permitted to

enter the exam room. Show your UDST ID Card before entering the exam room • If you did not bring your card, you must go to 9.1.80 to confirm your identification. • If this makes you late for your exam, you will not be permitted into the exam room. All personal belongings (bags, books, mobiles, etc.) will not be allowed in the exam room and must be left in your car. The following items are not permitted in exam rooms: • Smartphones • Smartwatches • Study notes or extra paper • Backpacks or gym bags • Purses or briefcases • Food or liquid Note: Food and/or liquid required for medical purposes is permitted with approval from the Admissions and Registration Directorate before your exam. Water may be permitted if it is in a clear plastic water bottle with the label removed (except during Ramadan). Once the exam begins, you cannot leave the exam until 30 minutes has passed. If you leave the exam room (i.e. illness, etc.), you will not be permitted back in the room. All exam papers must stay in the exam room after the exam. Students are required to sign the Exam Sign-In sheet before leaving the exam room. You may use a calculator if it is required for the exam and approved by your instructor. All bags/cases that carry pens, pencils, etc., must be made of clear plastic. If you have a question, please raise your hand and wait for the exam invigilator. No talking is permitted during the exam. If you are seen cheating or communicating to another student during an exam, you will receive zero on the exam and an Academic Dishonesty Incident Report will be filed on your permanent record. Letters and Records Graduation Ceremony Graduation Requirements Ramadan Schedule At UDST, many opportunities exist for students to be recognized through academic awards that reflect outstanding academic achievement. Many of these are presented at our annual Academic Awards Ceremony which is held in the Fall semester to honor recipients from the previous academic year. Some awards are presented in conjunction with our Graduation Ceremony, while others are non-ceremonial in nature and are formally recognized on a student's official transcript.Eligible students are selected for the below awards based on the outlined criteria without prior application. Ceremonial Awards Academic Excellence Award (Master Degree) Awarded to a FINAL

YEAR Master's degree student with the highest weighted average from the previous academic year at the University. Academic Excellence Award (Bachelor Degree) Awarded to a FINAL YEAR Bachelor degree student with the highest weighted average for the previous academic year at the University. Academic Excellence Award (Advanced Diploma) Awarded to a FINAL YEAR Advanced Diploma student with the highest weighted average for the previous academic year at the University. Academic Excellence Award (Two Year Diploma) Awarded to a FINAL YEAR Diploma student with the highest weighted average for the previous academic year at the University. Academic Merit Award Awarded to the two undergraduate students with the highest weighted average from each College after the selection of the Academic Excellence Awards recipients. Academic Distinction Award Awarded to the top six undergraduate students with the highest weighted average from each College after selection of the Academic Excellence and Academic Merit Awards recipients. Foundation Program Award Awarded to four students from the Foundation Program in recognition of their academic achievements and personal growth, as nominated by the Foundation Program Academic Manager. Industrial Trades Dean Award Awarded to five students from the Industrial Trades programs in recognition of their academic achievements as nominated by the Dean of College of Engineering and Technology. Achievement Award Awarded to eligible students in order to recognize their achievement in improving their English language skills and exceling in their study till the point of graduation. Applied Research Award Awarded to the nominated student based on participation in internal or external research programs presentation of research at National and/or international events, and/or the number of publications resulting from engagements with research projects. Innovation Award Awarded to the nominated students based on an innovative Capstone Project, Business Case, Entrepreneurial Proposal, internal or external innovation competitions where the student represented the University. Best Student Research on Countering Terrorism Awarded to a student who has made the best research on countering terrorism- as part of an institution strives to promote the local society’s peace and security. Non-Ceremonial Awards Dean's List The Dean's list is a notation recorded on eligible students' official transcripts for Fall & Winter Semesters. The Awardees will also have their name published on the UDST website.To be eligible, students will need to meet the following criteria:1. Student must have passed all courses in the semester2. Student must have a Term GPA of 4.003. Student must be a full-time credit student in the semester Graduation Awards Graduate with Honors Graduates with the below outlined cumulative GPA at the time of the Graduation Ceremony will receive the following recognition: Graduates with cumulative GPA of 4.00 will receive a Highest Honors notation, a trophy, and a Gold Cord as part of their Graduation regalia. Graduates with cumulative GPA of 3.80 to 3.99 will receive a High Honors notation and a Silver Cord as prat of their Graduation regalia. Graduates with cumulative GPA of 3.50 to 3.79 will receive an Honors notation and a Silver Cord as part of their Graduation regalia. President's Award

for Academic Excellence Awarded to one graduate from each program who attains the highest weighted average in their program, with a minimum cumulative GPA of 3.60. Criteria for Academic Awards No application is required for the above outlined Academic Awards. Eligible students will be automatically selected/nominated based on each outlined award criteria. No monetary award will be given to a candidate who holds an award of equal or greater value unless specifically required by the terms of the award. Certain conditions apply. There are different eligibility criteria for awarding academic awards. Full information about the eligibility criteria for each academic award is available in the Academic Catalog. Academic Awards FAQs What awards are presented at the Academic Awards Ceremony? The following awards are presented during the annual Academic Awards Ceremony:Academic Excellence AwardsAcademic Merit AwardAcademic Distinction AwardFoundation Program AwardIndustrial Trades Dean AwardAchievement AwardApplied Research AwardInnovation AwardBest Student Research on Countering Terrorism When is the Academic Awards Ceremony? The Academic Awards Ceremony is usually held in the Fall semester in the month of November. The official date for the ceremony will be announced on campus. Who decides which awards will be offered? Awards administered by the University are decided upon the recommendations of the Awards Committee. How are award recipients determined? Most awards are awarded based on a calculated weighted average, while some are awarded based on nomination. Unless otherwise stated, applications are not required in order to be considered for academic awards. If an application is required, it will be communicated to students, and application forms will be available at the Admissions & Registration Directorate. Who is eligible for an Academic Award? To be eligible for most academic awards, a student must be currently enrolled in a recognized University program and have completed at least two semesters (excluding Spring/Summer) in the previous academic year as a full-time student. Returning graduates are only eligible if they are currently enrolled and have completed at least two semesters (excluding Spring/Summer) in the previous academic year as a full-time student in the same program. There are different eligibility criteria for each award. Full information about the eligibility criteria for each academic award is available in the Academic Catalog. How can I apply for an Academic Award? No application is required for academic awards, as eligible students will be automatically selected/nominated based on each outlined award criteria. Why have I not received an award even though I have met all criteria? Students are selected based on the outlined criteria; however, there are limited quantities for each award. Successful students will be notified once they have been selected as an award recipient. Can a student receive multiple Academic Awards? No monetary award will be given to a candidate who holds an award of equal or greater value, unless specifically required by the terms of the award. Certain conditions apply. What is the minimum weighted average that is considered? For awards based on weighted average, a minimum of 80% weighted average applies. How do you calculate weighted average?

A student’s weighted average can be defined as the sum of a course grade multiplied by the course value (credit), divided by the sum of the course values (all credits).For Academic Awards presented in the Fall semester during the annual Academic Awards Ceremony, only the courses from the previous academic year will be included in the weighted average calculation.For returning graduates, courses completed in the graduated program are not included in the weighted average calculation. Are Academic Awards printed on transcripts? Academic Awards administered by the University will be recorded on the recipient’s academic transcript. Who is eligible to attend the Graduation Ceremony for Honors Students? Students receiving the below awards/recognition are eligible to attend the Graduation Ceremony for Honors Students: President's Award for Academic Excellence: Awarded to one graduate from each program who has the highest weighted average. Please note this award is not based on GPA. Graduates with Honors: For graduates with a minimum cumulative GPA of 3.50. Eligible students will be assigned to this ceremony, which will be identified during Ceremony Registration. Am I eligible to attend the College Ceremony even though I am an Honors Student? Students are assigned to a Graduation Ceremony based on their cumulative GPA and can only attend their assigned ceremony. Graduates are not permitted to attend multiple ceremonies. Is there an award ceremony for Dean's List recipients? Dean's List recognition is noted on the official transcripts of eligible students, and the names of awardees are published on the University's website. The recipients will also be recognized at a College-level celebration. Social media links Twitter Youtube Instagram Facebook Linkedin Graduation Ceremony 2025 Navigation Home Before the Ceremony Day of Ceremony After the Ceremony Graduation Ring FAQ Congratulations, Class of 2025, on your academic achievements. We look forward to celebrating with you! #UDSTGrad25 This year we will have three graduation ceremonies which will be held at the Qatar National Convention Centre (QNCC) and live-streamed on YouTube. Ceremony dates are as follows: Graduation Class of 2025 ceremonies date Ceremony for Honors Students May 13, 2025 College of Business and College of Computing and Information Technology May 14, 2025 College of Engineering and Technology and College of Health Sciences May 15, 2025 Watch the live stream below YouTube Embed - 13 May Graduation Ceremony for Honors Students YouTube Embed - 14 May Graduation Ceremony of College

of Business and College of Computing and Information Technology YouTube Embed - 15 May Graduation Ceremony of College of Health Sciences and College of Engineering and Technology Our Graduation ceremonies are the biggest highlight of the year and provide an excellent opportunity for you to celebrate your academic achievement with your family and friends. In preparation for the Graduation Ceremony, please read the following very carefully: Step 1: Registration Register your attendance for the Ceremony! Find out how to register for the Graduation Ceremony Asset 60 Copy Step 2: Graduation Gowns Find out what you need to know about: • Graduation Gown Pick-up • Graduation Gown Deposit • Graduation Gown Sizes Find out more Step 3: Invitations Find out details about the Ceremony invitation cards and when to collect them. Find out more Step 4: Graduation Rehearsal At Qatar National Convention Centre (QNCC) Rehearsal attendance is mandatory in order to participate in the Graduation Ceremony. You do not need your cap and gown for rehearsals. Please bring your student ID with you. Find out more Step 5: Graduation Day At Qatar National Convention Centre (QNCC) Find out more details about the Ceremony Days such as: • Ceremony Dates • Venue Location • When to Arrive • Dress Code Find out more Asset 65 Copy Step 6: Gown and Cap Return Dates You will sign out the cap and gown when you pick them up the week before your Graduation Ceremony. These must be returned during the period of: Sunday, June 1, 2025 - Thursday, June 5, 2025 Return Location: Building 16, ClubHub Time: Between 9:00 am and 3:00 pm Gown payment refunds will be made through bank transfer, not cash. To receive your refund, you must bring the receipt copy for the gown payment received during gown collection. Find out more Asset 58 Copy Step 7: Graduation Documents The graduation documents for 2025 graduates are expected to be available as follows: Fall 2024 Graduates: January 19, 2025 Winter 2025 Graduates: June 1, 2025 Spring 2025 Graduates: August 10, 2025 Graduates will be informed via email once the documents are ready for collection from the Admissions and Registration Directorate. Find out more Step 8: Register for the Alumni Association As a UDST Graduate, you can take advantage of a whole range of benefits and offers that are exclusive to you (including regular job postings!) Please register via the alumni portal to benefit from these offers and services. Make sure to also follow the alumni Instagram page to stay connected @UDST_Alumni Link to alumni portal Social media links Twitter Youtube Instagram Facebook Linkedin UDST - Qatar Al Tarafa, Jelaiah Street Duhail North P.O. Letters and Records Graduation Ceremony Graduation Requirements Ramadan Schedule It is the student’s responsibility to ensure that all program graduation requirements have been met. Submission of the Apply for Graduation form by the required deadlines ensures that the Admissions and Registration Directorate will have the opportunity to review the student’s program requirements and complete an audit. Students should be aware that courses not required for their program will not be used to calculate their final Cumulative GPA. Graduation Requirements Students must meet the following criteria in order to be eligible to graduate: Successfully complete all courses pertaining to their program plan Achieve clear academic standing Achieve

a minimum GPA of 2.00 for undergraduate programs and 3.00 for graduate programs Clear all outstanding fees Meet their English Proficiency Graduation Requirement for their program where required. Return all University equipment and books Applying to Graduate Students must submit an application to Graduate via the Student Self-Service portal by the published deadline to be considered for graduation and to receive their Credentials.Please refer to the Graduation Application Manual for detailed instructions on how to apply for graduation through the Student Self-Service portal.FALL SEMESTERCredential ConferralJanuary 25Deadline to apply for graduationOctober 15WINTER SEMESTERCredential ConferralJune 14Deadline to apply for graduationFebruary 15SPRING SEMESTERCredential ConferralAugust 9Deadline to apply for graduationMarch 15 Important to Note Students must apply for graduation to receive their program certificate. Students must have completed or be enrolled in all remaining courses in the Spring semester to be eligible to attend the Graduation Ceremony. If a student is graduating from multiple programs, a separate graduation application must be submitted to the Admissions and Registration Directorate for each program. There is only one official Graduation Ceremony, held in Spring. Social media links Twitter Youtube Instagram Facebook Linkedin UDST - Qatar Al Tarafa, Jelaiah Street Duhail North P.O. Symplicity Social media links Twitter Youtube Instagram Facebook Linkedin Continuing and Professional Education Register Today Welcome to Continuing and Professional Education We are committed to responding to your technical and vocational training needs by providing you wtih a range of value-added and innovative technical and professional training programs. As part of our ongoing support to Qatar National Vision 2030, we established strategic partnerships with international educational providers to offer high demand training to our clients such as American Heart Association (AHA); American Management Association (AMA); Chartered Institute of Personnel and Development (CIPD); CISCO; Microsoft; National Examination Board in Occupational Safety and Health (NEBOSH) courses; and Petroskills to name a few. CPE takes pride in its strong relationship with the industry and we persistently aim to respond to their various corporate requirements. This

led us to the launch of our consultancy services in various fields including strategic management; feasibility analysis; quality management; technological innovation; operations management; instructional design; business development and expansion; financial analysis. Our courses are designed to meet the unique needs of your organization and support the achievement of your individual professional development goals. We offer Asset 58 Copy Dynamic and flexible courses Asset 80 Copy Relevant and practical training time zone Convenient scheduling Start-of-the-art facilities at UDST (including smart classrooms, computer systems, industrial workshops and laboratories) Asset 89 Copy Facilitators of the highest quality Asset 1 Copy Expertise from UDST's diverse faculty time zone Local, national and international experts CPE Course Catalog 2026 CLICK HERE Our areas of expertise include: Asset 3 Copy Business Management Asset 80 Copy Engineering and Technology. noun-languages-skill-3683790 EN English Language Skills Asset 2 Copy Health Sciences Asset 4 Copy Computing and Information Technology Asset 89 Copy Leadership Development Highlighted Partners PetroSkills PetroSkills courses are built on extensive real-world experience in every corner of the oil and gas value chain. It combines the insights from its Alliance member companies with the knowledge of seasoned industry experts to help with your most complex challenges. From talent development and organizational performance to engineering and operational needs, PetroSkills is driven by one goal: improve your performance. View More AMA Certified Professional in Management (AMA-CPMTM) AMA-CPMTM is a third-party attestation of an individual's level of knowledge or proficiency in a certain industry or profession. Managers must not only have skills, capabilities, and competence to lead, but the character to positively impact and influence people. AMA-CPM is a standard for determining who is qualified to meet these criteria. The target audience is both current managers and potential managers. View More Chartered Institute of Personnel Development (CIPD) Qualifications Experts in the world of work and career partner to over 160,000 members around the world. In partnership with Bradfield Learning and Development, a CIPD-approved center since 1992 and a top brand across the Gulf region, CPE is now able to offer several accredited CIPD courses of study to its Corporate Clients View more CISCO As a worldwide leader in IT and networking, the certifications that participants obtain from Cisco will prepare them for exciting roles in IT world. Skills that are obtained from the different programs include networking, programmable infrastructure, cybersecurity, programming, best practices and operating systems and IT. Microsoft Learn for Educators (MSLE) Program The Microsoft Learn for Educators (MSLE) – Educational Institution program offers educational institutions with quality training resources and certifications on Microsoft technologies to help institutions’ educators as well as its students obtain the skills needed to reach their academic and career potential. American Heart Association (AHA) As the primary AHA International Training Center in Qatar, UDST provides certification in Basic Life Support (BLS), Advanced Cardiac Life Support (ACLS) and Pediatric Advanced Life Support (PALS). NEBOSH This certification stands for National Examination Board in Occupational Safety and Health. As a gold learning partner institution, UDST provides certification that offers

professional qualification in health, safety and environmental management. Department of Healthcare Professionals (DHP) UDST is one of the Academic Institutions in Qatar that are accredited by the Ministry of Public Health’s Department of Healthcare Professions to provide CME/ CPE activities to licensed healthcare practitioners in Qatar. FAQs How to register? Online registration is now available through UDST's CPE Online Registration Portal: https://apps.udst.edu.qa/corpa Who can take a CPE course or certificate program? Are there any education or job-related prerequisites? Generally speaking, anyone with a keen desire to learn and enhance their knowledge is welcome into our fold. However, based on the kind of course that is being applied to, certain pre-requisites may apply. For instance; CIPD certification is reserved exclusively for HR Professionals, The O&G courses that we conduct in partnership Petroskills is reserved for technical staff that operate in O&G organisations and CPD training is aimed solely at Healthcare practitioners with a current license in Qatar. How long does the average course or certificate program take to complete? Duration can range between a few hours for some of our courses to a few months for some of our certificate programs. What is CPE’s refund policy? Category Eligibility for refund A- Learners must notify CPE of their intent to withdraw or cancel at least 6 business days prior to the start date of a course, program, or CPD activity to be entitled to a full refund. In the case of American Heart Association (AHA) courses which require that learners purchase a key (online access) to access online learning resources two weeks in advance of the start date of their course, the learner must notify CPE of their intention to withdraw or cancel at least 15 business days prior to the start of a course. B- If the learner notifies the office with the intent to withdraw or cancel less than 6 days for all courses, programs, or CPD activities except AHA which is 15 days prior to the course, program, or CPD activity start date, respectively, the learner will forfeit their tuition fees. C- A learner who registers for a course, program, or CPD activity, who does not notify the University of their intent to withdraw, and who does not attend any classes, will be deemed a "no show" and will forfeit their tuition fees. D- UDST reserves the right to cancel any training course in advance of the scheduled course date. In this case, you will have the option to reschedule for a future course or to receive a full refund. To receive your refund, you must submit a completed refund form within 90 days of the canceled course date. links Twitter Youtube Instagram Facebook Linkedin Professional Education Consulting Services Consulting Services UDST provides a variety of consulting services catering to businesses, industries, government bodies, and non-profit organizations that are interested in independent expertise. Our consultants, drawn from UDST's five Colleges, are renowned for their research that directly applies to various real-world contexts. Working closely with clients, our consultants offer academic insights that assist organizations in problem-solving and improving their operational capacities. Additionally, our newly inaugurated Central Analysis Lab, provides an array of testing solutions, including chromatography, elemental and molecular spectroscopy, and materials testing for construction. Tailored analysis requests are accommodated, adhering to rigorous protocols and international standards like ASTM, ISO, and EPA. Our academic professionals specialize in both conventional and specialized domains, including (but not limited to) : - Oil

& Gas - Engineering Technology - Strategic Management - Operations Management - Process Improvement - Financial Advisory - Human Resources - Information Technology - Health Services and Management What are the advantages of utilizing our consulting services? Engaging with us offers access to cutting-edge research, impartial and unbiased analysis, and opportunities for cross-disciplinary collaboration. Our adept team of project managers can link your organization with the appropriate expertise and services from all UDST faculties and academic departments. As the First National Applied University offering applied Bachelor’s degrees and Master’s degrees in addition to certificates and diplomas in various fields, our consultants combine academic credentials and intellectual rigor with hands-on experience and real-world insights. Each consultant is a leader in their respective fields, actively involved in ongoing research and at the forefront of professional practice. skills that will propel them to successful careers across Qatar and the Globe. We have seen so many of you excel in your fields and reach new heights, which instils a sense of pride in the UDST community. UDST’s Alumni are its greatest asset and one of the reasons for

its success as an Institution of Excellence for Technical and Vocational Education and Training. You are an inspiration to the University’s current students and no matter what year you graduated in or where you are in the world right now, rest assured that UDST will always be your home. University of Doha for Science and Technology is eager to hear from you and the Alumni Office is ready to provide you with any information you need about your privileges and benefits as a University graduate. Alumni Office Graduation Ceremonies Update Contact Info To update the alumni contact information send an email to alumni@udst.edu.qa requesting an update of contact information. Update Contact Info The Admissions and Registration Department provides a number of different documents for graduates for a variety of purposes. These documents and their descriptions are noted in the below table. Students must make a formal request for any one of these documents at the Admissions and Registration Department and should expect a 2-5 day turnaround, based on the type of document requested. Type of Letter Description Availability of the Letter Graduation Letter A letter issued to graduating

students and alumni that indicates student details, academic program graduated, GPA, language of instruction, and year of graduation. 3 working days Official Transcript An official document that includes details of the student’s academic record since the first day of enrollment at the College. 3 working days Diploma/Degree Attested Copy A copy of the diploma/degree that is stamped and verified to be an identical copy of the original. 3 working days Parchment Replacement A replacement of parchment issued upon a graduate’s verification that the original parchment has been lost, stolen, or destroyed. 3 working days Course Outline A document that includes a detailed description of the course undertaken by the graduate at the college during the period of study. Course outlines should be sent by email directly from the College to the educational institution(s) requesting the course outlines. 3 working days Request Letters & Records & Request Transcript Alumni who wish to submit a request to get an information about their studies, program, records, official transcript, graduation documents, authentications, and other services can do the following. All fields that the user must fill in for the Request Letters & Records All alumni are able to login to their account. The following services can be submitted / processed online Graduation Services: Students can apply for graduation letter / Parchment replacement/ Parchment authentication through the following services: Requesting the action online through e-form service Send an email to Grad.Inquiries@udst.edu.qa How To submit an e-Form Login to My UDST using your alumni account. Click on STUDENT EFORMS. Select Requested Letters, Records or Transcript from the dropdown list. Complete all required fields and Submit. My UDST Login Request Letters & Records Alumni who wish to submit a request but not in the above-mentioned navigation/guides should follow the below information. Read More IT Services Alumni Email for life is available to all graduates of UDST and provides a lifelong email address. Your alumni email account will have a default address in the following format 60XXXXXX@udst.edu.qa . If you have any difficulty logging in, please contact the IT helpdesk via email ithelpdesk@udst.edu.qa or call 4495 2470 For more information about or assistance with UDST alumni email accounts, please contact the Alumni Specialist alumni@udst.edu.qa . Instagram Facebook Linkedin Testing Centre Book Placement Tests About the TC In January 2007, the University of Doha (UDST) established the UDST Testing Centre. The Testing Centre was designed to address the testing needs of UDST in the areas of entrance, placement, and exit testing for its regular programs, as well as for Corporate Services. It also serves the larger community as an approved center for the administration of the International English Language Testing System (IELTS) test, in addition to providing proctoring services for institutions outside Qatar. The Testing Centre is located on the second floor of the Administration Building (Building 3). There are five test rooms, three of which have computer labs, eight oral interview rooms, administrative offices, a reception and two waiting areas for clients. All

assessments delivered through the TC are secure exams. This means there are strict guidelines and procedures in place to protect the security and integrity of tests and to ensure all candidates receive fair and equal treatment. For the standards and practices the Testing Centre strives to attain, please see Principles of Good Practice for ALTE Examinations at www.alte.org. Placement Tests Test Dates Fees Guideline (when to arrive, what to bring) Preparation (resources – test format, study guide) IELTS Tests Registration and Test Dates Fees Guideline (when to arrive, what to bring) Preparation (resources – test format) Proctoring Availability Fees Guideline (when to arrive, what to bring) Proctoring Details The Testing Centre provides professional and secure proctoring services to those individuals studying at educational institutions outside Qatar and are required to take exams while in Doha. Regular proctoring services can start at 8:30 a.m., with all testing to be completed by 3:00 p.m. Written requests for all proctoring services must be made a minimum of 10 working days in advance. Alternative dates for proctoring services may be available, if scheduling permits. Unless the institute has a long standing billing agreement set up with UDST, a proctoring fee of 200 QR per test is required to be paid on the scheduled test day by the candidate. Courier costs may also apply. If a candidate wishes to reschedule their exam, in two or less working days without a valid medical note or police report, an additional fee will be applied. Candidates should arrive 30 minutes before an exam start time. Candidates should confirm with their exam provider materials that are allowed in the exam room. Academic English Placement The Academic English Placement (AEP) tool is an online adaptive test designed to assess an applicant's English language ability. The test results are used to place students in classes at UDST. Applicants requiring additional language support depending on eligibility and availability may be eligible to take English language classes at UDST to ensure future success in their program of study. Please click here to see the format of the Academic English Placement Test (you may need to use a laptop or computer to see this). Frequently Asked Questions: How do I schedule a placement test? During the application process, candidates will reach Step 12, where they must book placement test(s) before paying the application fees, validating, and submitting the application. Candidates will have multiple options for test dates and times to choose from. For assistance, candidates can visit the Admissions Office to check the application and receive support with booking test(s). What do I need to bring? Candidates must bring their original passport or national ID card. No photocopies will be accepted. Without proper ID, candidates will not be permitted to write the test. All test materials including writing materials will be provided by the UDST Testing Centre. No calculators or personal items are allowed in the test room. What time should I arrive to write the test? Candidates must be at the UDST Testing

Centre thirty (30) minutes before the scheduled test time. Where do I get my test results? Test results will be emailed within five (5) business days. Can I retake the AEP? Yes, candidates can retake the AEP but must wait 30 days before taking a new test. Candidates should take the time between tests to improve their English. For each test series taken, the 200 QR test fee will apply. Academic Math Placement The Academic Math Placement (AMP) is a test that measures a candidate's current mathematical skills. The results help to advise candidates on the appropriate mathematics course in which to enroll in order to complete the mathematics requirement for a particular program of study. At UDST, there are two parts to the AMP: AMP 1 and AMP 2. AMP 1 determines a candidate's math admission score to the university. AMP 2 determines whether or not a candidate can be exempted from undergraduate math courses. Please review the diagram and explanation below to understand all the important information. Academic Math Placement 1 (AMP 1) AMP 1 is a test of basic mathematics skills to measure a candidate's proficiency in specific areas of high school math. All candidates applying to UDST must register for AMP 1. Candidates can only be exempt from writing AMP 1 if they have advanced standing for credit earned in previous studies based on an acceptable score to UDST. Registering for the Test During the application process, candidates will reach Step 12, where they must book placement test(s) before paying the application fees, validating, and submitting the application. Candidates will have multiple options for test dates and times to choose from. For assistance, candidates can visit the Admissions Office to check the application and receive support with booking test(s). Preparing for the Test To prepare for the test, UDST provides a study guide Click Here. This guide helps identify any weaknesses in certain math skills to improve upon before taking the test. The internet is also a great source of practice problems. Test Topics Real Numbers Functions Fractions Decimals Formula Rearrangement Percent Solving Equations Word Problems Laws of Exponents Whole Numbers and Integers Negative Exponents Polynomials Factoring Data Measurement Rational Expressions Geometry Equation of Straight Lines Logarithms Trigonometry Systems of Equations & Inequalities Test Details AMP 1 is a computer-based test consisting of 60 multiple-choice questions. The time limit is two hours. Candidates must arrive 30 minutes before the scheduled test time with an original, valid passport or national ID card. No photocopies are accepted. Without proper ID, the test cannot be written. The UDST Testing Centre provides all test materials including a calculator (Casio fx- 991ES PLUS), formula sheet, and writing materials. All personal items must remain outside the test room. Test results are emailed within five business days after the test date. Special accommodations are available for those with special needs. It is a candidate's responsibility to self-identify their needs and provide valid documentation at the time of application. A university counsellor reviews the

documentation to determine what testing accommodations can be provided. Failure to notify UDST of the need for testing accommodation does not permit a re-test. Test Results AMP 1 Score Course Registration Requirement Low* Preparatory Mathematics Pass MATH1010 or MATH1070 High Pass Register for AMP Part 2 *Candidates who receive a score of low may register for and attempt AMP 1 again after thirty (30) days. A 200 QR fee applies. Academic Math Placement 2 (AMP 2) AMP 2 is a test of advanced skills in mathematics to measure a student’s proficiency in areas of advanced algebra and functions, analytical trigonometry, and other precalculus topics. Only students with a high pass score on AMP 1 are eligible to write AMP 2. Registering for the Test For those who are eligible, a test will be automatically scheduled. An invitation email will be sent, confirming the test date, time, and location. There is no fee to write AMP 2, and only one attempt is permitted. Appointments are available during set testing periods. For assistance regarding the AMP 2, candidates may contact the Testing Centre at [email protected]. Preparing for the Test To prepare for the test, UDST recommends a thorough review of advanced math topics from two main resources. Electronic copies are available for purchase at the UDST bookstore or from online bookshops. Miller, O’Neill and Hyde. (2018). Intermediate Algebra (6th Ed.). ISBN13:9781264859283 Miller, J. and Gerken, D. (2018). Precalculus (2nd ed). ISBN13: 9780078035609 Test Topics Trigonometric Functions Trigonometric Expressions Trigonometric Equations Radian & Degree Measure Absolute Value & Radical Equations Polynomial Functions Logarithmic Functions Exponential Functions Inverse Functions The Circle Applications of Functions Even and Odd Functions Graphical Analysis of Functions Transformations of Functions Introduction to Linear Algebra Asymptotic Behavior Complex Numbers Rational Equations Linear Inequalities The Parabola Test Details AMP 2 is a computer-based test consisting of 60 multiple-choice questions. The time limit is two hours. Candidates must arrive 30 minutes before the scheduled test time with an original, valid passport or national ID card. No photocopies are accepted. Without proper ID, the test cannot be written. The UDST Testing Centre provides all test materials including calculator (Casio fx- 991ES PLUS) and writing materials. A formula sheet is neither required nor permitted during AMP 2. All personal items must remain outside the test room. Test results are emailed within five business days after the test date. Special accommodations permitted for AMP 1 also apply to AMP 2. Test Results AMP 2 Score Course Exemption Course Registration Requirement Low None MATH1010 or MATH1070 Medium MATH1010 or MATH1070 MATH1020 High MATH1010 and MATH1020 or MATH1070 MATH1030 Amenities include: Friendly and knowledgeable registration staff Male and female waiting room Classrooms and labs that meet international testing standards Private oral testing rooms Modern testing software We are also an official testing site for the International English Language Testing System (IELTS) Test. These tests are open to the broader community as well as UDST students. Social media links Twitter Youtube Instagram Facebook Linkedin UDST - Qatar

Instagram Facebook Linkedin IELTS Tests Responsive Video IELTS measures the ability to communicate in English across all four language skills (listening, reading, writing, and speaking). UDST is an approved venue for IELTS tests, which are recognised at over 10,000 institutions worldwide. Whether you're taking the IELTS for work, university or migration, we are here to help you achieve your goals! IELTS Trial Test We are pleased to announce that we are now offering free IELTS Trial Tests! Taking the IELTS Trial Test helps familiarise yourself with taking the test under test conditions, and can also help identify where to improve. For more information, or to register, click the button below Register now! UDST Offers Different Types of IELTS Academic Academic IELTS is for those who want to study

at a higher education institution where English is the main language of instruction. General Training General Training IELTS is for work experience, to take training programs, or migrate to an English-speaking country. UKVI If you want to work, migrate, study or seek vocational training in the UK, you might need to take the IELTS for UKVI test. It is a Secure English Language Test (SELT) approved by the UK home office for visa applications to the United Kingdom. All UKVI tests will be delivered on Computer, and we offer both Academic and General Training IELTS for UKVI. Taking Paper-based IELTS at UDST: Online reservation for up to 48 hours Online or in-person payment with a bank card Small test rooms with a maximum of 30 candidates Good quality sound system for the listening test (no headphones) All writing utensils and water supplied by UDST All parts of the test are done on the same day Results available within 13 days by SMS Taking IELTS on Computer at UDST (including UKVI): Online reservation for up to 48 hours In-person payment with a bank card Small test rooms with a maximum of 25 candidates Individual headphones provided for the listening test All writing utensils and water supplied by UDST All parts of the test are done on the same day Results available in 1-5 days by SMS, and online More Information about IELTS at UDST Go through these tabs for more information regarding registering for the IELTS, making any payments and getting your results. Registration Fees Test Day There are two ways to register for an IELTS at UDST: online and in person. To find a test date and register online, go to IELTS Online Registration To Register in Person: Visit the UDST Testing Centre in person. We are located on the 2nd floor of Building 3. If you need instructions on how to get there, visit this link: How to get to UDST? To register for IELTS, you will need: An original, valid Identity Document (passport OR Qatar National ID). Please note that the details (including name and ID number) on your TRF will match the details on the Identity Document you provide. These cannot be changed once payment has been received. For UKVI tests, all candidates must bring an original Passport. Qatar National ID cards are not accepted for UKVI tests. Photocopies, photos, expired or cancelled passports are not accepted. On the Test Day, you must bring the same identification document (passport or QID) that you used to register Office Hours for Registration: Sunday-Thursday 7:30 am – 2:30 pm. Testing Centre, University Of Doha For Science and Technology, Building 3, 2nd Floor. For Data Protection: Please click here for information on how IELTS and Cambridge use and store personal data collected during registration. It is important that your privacy and personal data are protected. If you have any questions about such matters, you can contact data.protection@cambridgeassessment.org.uk .
